questions.question: Why did the Mughal Empire decline in the 18th century?
questions.correct_answer: Due to weak rulers, internal conflicts, and administrative problems
questions.explanation: These factors allowed regional powers to gain independence and grow stronger.

questions.question: Name some important regional powers during the 18th century.
questions.explanation: These states expanded their influence as Mughal power weakened.

questions.question: How did European trading companies influence India?
questions.correct_answer: By setting up trading posts and interfering in local politics
questions.explanation: The British and French companies used alliances and military support to increase their power.

questions.question: What was the significance of the Battle of Plassey?
questions.correct_answer: It allowed the British East India Company to gain control over Bengal
questions.explanation: Victory in the battle gave the British political and economic influence in India.

questions.question: What was the outcome of the Battle of Buxar?
questions.correct_answer: It strengthened British control in eastern India and reduced Mughal influence
questions.explanation: The victory helped the British expand their territory and revenue collection.

questions.question: How did regional rulers administer their states?
questions.correct_answer: They collected revenue, maintained armies, and managed law and order
questions.explanation: Administrative systems varied, but local officials helped rulers maintain control.

questions.question: What role did alliances play among regional powers?
questions.correct_answer: They strengthened positions and sometimes led to wars or conflicts
questions.explanation: Alliances were strategic, often involving European companies to gain advantage.

questions.question: How did European expansion impact regional powers?
questions.correct_answer: It reduced their independence and set the stage for British colonial rule
questions.explanation: Territorial control and military interventions by Europeans weakened local rulers.

questions.question: How did political changes affect daily life in India?
questions.correct_answer: Changes in rulers and policies affected taxation, trade, and local governance
questions.explanation: Political stability or instability influenced the lives of farmers, traders, and citizens.

questions.question: Give an example of how the British East India Company gained power.
questions.correct_answer: By supporting local rulers in battles and gaining control of territories like Bengal
questions.explanation: Military support and strategic alliances allowed the company to expand its influence.

notes.title: Mughal Decline
notes.content: Weak rulers, internal conflicts, and administrative issues led to the weakening of Mughal authority.

notes.title: Regional Powers
notes.content: Marathas, Sikhs, Nizams, and Rajputs became strong and formed alliances or rivalries.

notes.title: European Companies
notes.content: British and French companies influenced Indian politics using trade, alliances, and military support.

notes.title: Major Battles
notes.content: Plassey (1757) and Buxar (1764) were significant in establishing British political and economic control.

notes.title: Administration
notes.content: Regional rulers managed taxation, law, and military through local officials, which varied from Mughal systems.

facts.fact: The Mughal Empire weakened in the 18th century due to internal problems.
Marathas, Sikhs, Nizams, and Rajputs were the major regional powers.
The British East India Company gained power after the Battle of Plassey.
Alliances between regional powers and European companies were often strategic and temporary.
Political changes affected taxation, trade, and governance, impacting everyday life.
